observations.label: Memory palace has 4 layers: Palace → Wing → Room → Locus
observations.description: The memory palace is structured as a 4-layer navigable hierarchy: Palace (whole system), Wing (top-level module, 4 total), Room (functional cluster, 7 today), Locus (single promoted file).
observations.evidence: Palace — the whole system. One index page. Wing — a top-level module. 4 wings total. Room — a functional cluster inside a wing. 7 rooms today. Locus — a single file promoted into a room.

observations.label: AI must translate questions to concerns before navigating rooms
observations.description: AI agents must first translate user questions into concerns, then navigate to matching rooms, verify ownership via purpose line, and only then access core loci files.
observations.evidence: 1. Translate the user's question into a concern. 2. Open the matching room page. 3. Read the purpose line — does this room actually own the concern? 4. Look at the core loci table.

observations.label: Locus rank formula: inbound*2 + outbound + critical*10 + entry*3
observations.description: Files are promoted into rooms based on a composite score of inbound references (×2), outbound references (×1), criticality (×10), and entry-point status (×3).

observations.label: Navigate palace by concern → room → purpose → core loci
observations.description: The prescribed action sequence for AI codebase navigation: identify concern, open matching room, verify ownership, read core loci table.
observations.evidence: 1. Translate the user's question into a concern. 2. Open the matching room page. 3. Read the purpose line. 4. Look at the core loci table.

observations.label: Enable AI agents to navigate codebase without blind file scanning
observations.description: The memory palace exists so AI agents can find relevant files through structured navigation rather than grep-scanning the entire codebase.
observations.evidence: The memory palace at 10_Wiki/palace/ is a navigable, room-based view of the codebase. This file tells AI agents how to use it.

observations.label: Each room page contains Purpose, Core Loci, Traversal, Raw Paths
observations.description: Room pages follow a standardized structure with four sections that serve as navigation signals for AI agents.
observations.evidence: Purpose — one sentence naming the concern. Core Loci — ranked table. Traversal — links back to wing, palace, retrieval rules. Raw Paths — plaintext file paths.

relationships.description: The retrieval constraint governs how navigation actions must be performed.
The 4-layer structure enables the concern→room→loci navigation pattern.
The ranking formula produces the locus promotions that populate rooms.
The navigation goal requires the retrieval constraint to prevent blind scanning.

missing_information: No information about palace update frequency or staleness detection
explicit_unknowns: Whether all 7 rooms are currently populated

entry_point_invoked.note: Used contract classes directly. No automated decomposer exists — decomposition is manual using the canonical primitive types.
